summary: Completed security hardening tests for 2026 production. All 15 backend tests passed. Fixed frontend compilation error in ComprehensiveLevelTest.js (duplicate API_URL declaration). All security features verified working: bcrypt password hashing with SHA-256 migration, /auth/direct-reset endpoint removed, reading questions served without 'correct' field, server-side reading evaluation, upload extension validation, vocabulary engine regression tests.

minor.issue: Response includes password_hash field (though value is null). Consider excluding from User model response.

seed_data_creation: Created temporary SHA-256 test user for migration testing (auto-cleaned)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security hardening features fully tested. Key endpoints: 1) POST /api/auth/login - bcrypt with SHA-256 fallback + migration working. 2) POST /api/auth/direct-reset - returns 404 (removed). 3) GET /api/level-test/reading-questions - returns questions WITHOUT 'correct' field. 4) GET /api/comprehensive-level-test/reading-questions - returns questions WITHOUT 'correct' field. 5) POST /api/comprehensive-level-test/evaluate-reading - server-side evaluation returns results with correct answers. 6) POST /api/payments/bank/upload - rejects .exe/.php, accepts .jpg/.png/.pdf. 7) Vocabulary engine regression tests pass.

bcrypt_password_hashing.details: SHA-256 hash migrated to bcrypt on successful login

reading_questions_no_answers.details: Both level-test and comprehensive-level-test reading endpoints return questions WITHOUT 'correct' field

server_side_reading_evaluation.details: POST /api/comprehensive-level-test/evaluate-reading evaluates answers server-side and returns results with correct answers

frontend_fix_applied.issue: Duplicate 'const API_URL' declaration on line 64 causing compilation error
